instrumentation.read: This opt-in DSv4 timing hook profiles the exact hot-expert weight broadcast and helper dW partial all-reduce path used by standard EP hot-helper execution. It synchronizes CUDA events per record, so it is a mechanism profile rather than a clean throughput baseline.

read.answer_to_hot_partial_reduce_question: The helper-rank partial dW reduction back to hot expert owners is already happening through _StandardEPHotWeightBroadcast.backward all_reduce(grad_hot_weight). Its owner scatter-add is tiny. The measured backward collective cost is real but not the dominant 12-layer bottleneck: rank-max is about 466 ms across two steps, roughly 233 ms per step, with w3 grad-hot-weight all-reduce as the noisy tail. This does not explain the multi-second MoE/EP gap by itself.
read.main_bottleneck_implication: The bigger MORI gap is integrating helper hot-row execution into the MORI dispatch -> AITER fused MoE -> MORI combine path without adding route-mask duplication, many tiny helper calls, dummy local experts, or unfused owner VJP work. For standard EP, hot dW all-reduce is a moderate communication cost, not the blocker that prevents helper execution from working.
read.next_mori_target: Keep the standard-EP hot-helper branch as the correctness/perf reference, then port the mechanism into MORI/AITER by batching helper rows and preserving AITER fused expert efficiency. If optimizing the existing standard-EP helper branch, target w3 grad-hot-weight all-reduce tail overlap or w13 fusion, not owner scatter-add.
